title: willughbeia auriculata
intro: Willughbeia auriculata, also known as the golden cat's claw, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to the island of New Caledonia and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to induce vomiting and act as a purgative. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, Willughbeia auriculata is often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
